subject: Sports
title: The Olympic Rings and Symbols
description: One-minute skill: The Olympic Rings and Symbols.

hook.text: Five interlocking rings on a white background—the most recognized symbol on Earth.

buildup.text: Coubertin designed the rings in 1913. The five rings represent the five inhabited continents.

discovery.text: Every nation's flag contains at least one of the six colors: blue, yellow, black, green, red, and white.

twist.text: The Olympic motto 'Citius, Altius, Fortius' (Faster, Higher, Stronger) added 'Communiter' (Together) in 2021.

climax.text: The torch, rings, anthem, and oath together form a ceremonial language understood worldwide.

punchline.text: Five rings unite every nation without a single word.

quiz.question: What do the five Olympic rings represent?
